name: XPT2046 Touch Screen Controller (Updated version)
description: The `xpt2046` touchscreen platform allows using the resistive touch screen controllers based on the XPT2046 chip ([datasheet](https://grobotronics.com/images/datasheets/xpt2046-datasheet.pdf), [AZ-Delivery](https://www.az-delivery.de/en/products/2-4-tft-lcd-touch-display)) with ESPHome. Many cheap LCD displays contain this controller. The [SPI](https://esphome.io/components/spi) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

config_entries.label: Update Interval
config_entries.description: The interval to check the sensor. If `interrupt_pin` is specified the touch will be detected nearly instantaneously and this setting will be used only for the release detection. Defaults to `50ms`.

config_entries.label: Calibration
config_entries.description: The XPT2046 is a resistive touch screen and it will require calibration on a per-device basis.

config_entries.label: X Max
config_entries.description: The raw value corresponding to the right

config_entries.label: X Min
config_entries.description: The raw value corresponding to the left

config_entries.label: Y Max
config_entries.description: The raw value corresponding to the bottom

config_entries.label: Y Min
config_entries.description: The raw value corresponding to the top

config_entries.label: CS Pin
config_entries.description: The chip select pin. Often marked `T_CS` on the board.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Interrupt Pin
config_entries.description: The touch detection pin. Often marked `T_IRQ` on the board. If not specified the component will use polling via SPI. This key is renamed from **irq_pin**

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Set the ID of this sensor.

config_entries.label: Threshold
config_entries.description: The value to detect the touch or release. Defaults to `400`.
